method_atom: A perturbative expansion of the stationary probability distribution of a Markov stochastic process (both jump and diffusion type) around a reference detailed-balance/reversible state, carried out to first order in a small driving parameter. The first-order correction to the reference distribution is identified with the finite transient part of the irreversible (time-antisymmetric) component of an action/flux functional, and the construction is linked to a transient fluctuation symmetry, to linear-response (correlation-based response) relations, and to a careful control of the order in which the stationary limit and the small-driving limit are taken.
object_atom: Nonequilibrium stationary distributions of mechanical/thermodynamic systems driven slightly out of thermal equilibrium, where the equilibrium Boltzmann distribution is corrected by the entropy flux, and notions such as local equilibrium and the Green-Kubo relation apply.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the order-controlled first-order expansion of a Markov process's steady state around a reversible reference using the irreversible flux functional and fluctuation symmetry; the domain-specific part is the physics of Boltzmann/entropy-flux nonequilibrium thermodynamics near equilibrium.
